stem: A right rectangular prism has a height of 9 inches. The length of the prism’s base is x inches, which is 7 inches more than the width of the prism’s base. Which function V gives the volume of the prism, in cubic inches, in terms of the length of the prism’s base?
explanation: The width is 7 less than the length, so it is x − 7. Volume equals length × width × height = x(x − 7)(9), which is V(x) = 9x(x − 7).
